lede: A look at April 18 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jochen Rindt and Audrey Tang, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Zimbabwe
events.desc: The Republic of Zimbabwe (formerly Rhodesia) comes into being, with Canaan Banana as the country's first President. The Zimbabwean dollar replaces the Rhodesian dollar as the official currency.

events.title: The Republic of Ireland Act 1948
events.desc: The Republic of Ireland Act comes into force, declaring Éire to be a republic and severing Ireland's "association" with the Commonwealth of Nations.

events.title: International Court of Justice
events.desc: The International Court of Justice holds its inaugural meeting in The Hague, Netherlands.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Doolittle Raid on Japan: Tokyo, Yokohama, Kobe and Nagoya are bombed.

events.title: Superman
events.desc: Superman debuts in Action Comics #1 (cover dated June 1938).

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: French pilot Roland Garros is shot down and glides to a landing on the German side of the lines.

events.title: Joan of Arc
events.desc: Joan of Arc is beatified in Rome.

events.title: Boston
events.desc: Bostonians rise up in rebellion against Sir Edmund Andros.

events.title: Martin Luther
events.desc: Trial of Martin Luther begins its second day during the assembly of the Diet of Worms. He refuses to recant his teachings despite the risk of excommunication.

events.title: Republic of Venice
events.desc: Peace of Ferrara between Republic of Venice, Duchy of Milan, Republic of Florence and House of Gonzaga: ending of the second campaign of the Wars in Lombardy fought until the Treaty of Lodi in 1454, which will then guarantee the conditions for the development of the Italian Renaissance.

births.name: Audrey Tang
births.line: Taiwanese Minister of Digital Affairs and programmer

births.name: Samuel P. Huntington
births.line: American political scientist, author, and academic

births.name: Tadeusz Mazowiecki
births.line: Polish journalist and politician, Prime Minister of Poland

births.name: Michael D. Higgins
births.line: Irish sociologist and politician, 9th President of Ireland

deaths.text: Albert Einstein, German-American physicist, engineer, and academic
Justus von Liebig, German chemist and academic

observances.text: Christian feast day: Cyril VI of Constantinople (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Blessed Marie of the Incarnation
